articles.author: The Associated Press
articles.description: As the war in the Middle East spirals further, U.S. President Donald Trump said on Monday that the U.S. has “the capability to go far longer” than its projected four-to-five-week time frame for its military operations against Iran.
articles.title: Live updates: Iran war likely to last 4 to 5 weeks, Trump says

articles.author: Bridget Brown, Lorian Belanger, Brian P. D. Hannon, Luena Rodriguez-Feo Vileira, Drew Callister, Lisa Marie Pane
articles.description: Across Iran’s capital, explosions rang out overnight as the U.S. and Israel pounded Iran with airstrikes. Iran and its allies have hit back against Israel and neighboring Gulf states.
articles.title: Live updates: Trump says ‘someone from within’ Iranian government may be best choice to take power

articles.author: Bridget Brown, Lorian Belanger, Brian P. D. Hannon, Luena Rodriguez-Feo Vileira, Drew Callister, Lisa Marie Pane
articles.description: Across Iran’s capital, explosions rang out overnight as the U.S. and Israel pounded Iran with airstrikes. Iran and its allies have hit back against Israel and neighboring Gulf states.

articles.author: Phil Holm, WILL JARRETT, Kevin Vineys
articles.description: Airstrikes destroy entire blocks and compounds. Missiles soar across borders. Drones rain lethal destruction.
articles.title: The US-Israeli war with Iran in maps, graphics and images

articles.author: Julia Frankel
articles.description: The unfolding war in the Middle East has ricocheted across the region. Nearly every country has sustained damage from missile hits or shrapnel. Many have reported casualties, and key economic engines and passageways have been closing down. Airspace is closed in many places. Even in countries where airspace is at least partially open, airlines have canceled flights. The U.S. State Department has advised Americans in 14 countries in the region to leave, even as commercial flights are hard to come by.
articles.title: How the unfolding war is affecting countries in the Middle East

articles.author: Fatima Hussein, Suman Naishadham
articles.description: President Donald Trump is threatening to cut off trade with Spain. He's citing a lack of support over the U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran and the European nation’s resistance to increase its NATO spending. Speaking in the Oval Office on Tuesday, Trump said “we don't want anything to do with Spain.” The U.S. president’s comments came a day after Spanish Foreign Minister José Manuel Albares said his country would not allow the U.S. to use the bases in southern Spain in any strikes not covered by the United Nations’ charter. It is unclear how Trump would cut off trade with Spain, given that Spain is under the umbrella of the European Union. The EU negotiates trade deals for all 27 member countries.
articles.title: Trump threatens to cut off trade with Spain

articles.author: Lisa Mascaro
articles.description: The Trump administration and its allies in Congress are presenting a shifting new justification for the U.S. attack on Iran. House Speaker Mike Johnson said late Monday after a briefing at the Capitol that the White House believed Israel was determined to act on its own, leaving President Donald Trump with a “very difficult decision.” Johnson said Trump and his team determined that Iran would immediately retaliate against U.S. personnel and assets. The remarkable turn of events comes as hostilities deepen across the region. The action killed Iran's supreme leader and has left hundreds dead. The president himself estimated the war could drag on for weeks.
articles.title: Trump officials say Israel helped lead US to Iran war

articles.author: Kelvin Chan
articles.description: Iranian drone strikes damaged three Amazon Web Services sites in the Middle East, exposing how vulnerable cloud data centers are in conflict. AWS said late Monday that drones directly hit two data centers in the United Arab Emirates and another site in Bahrain suffered damage after a drone landed nearby. AWS later said recovery work in the UAE was making progress. An expert said that Amazon typically configures its services so that the loss of a single data center would be relatively unimportant to its operations. He said the attacks are a reminder that cloud computing isn’t “magical” and still requires physical facilities that are vulnerable to disaster scenarios.
articles.title: Strikes on Amazon data centers highlights vulnerability to physical risks

articles.author: Samuel Petrequin
articles.description: French President Emmanuel Macron has France’s nuclear-powered aircraft carrier to move from the Baltic Sea to the Mediterranean to protect allied assets during the ongoing war in the Middle East. Macron said the Charles de Gaulle will be escorted by its air wing, and its escorting frigates. In a pre-recorded speech on French TV, Macron added that Rafale fighter jets, air-defense systems, and airborne radar systems have been deployed over the past few hours in the Middle East.
articles.title: Macron orders France's nuclear-powered aircraft carrier to the Mediterranean
